description: Confounder: The model assumes token_score_sums[indices] equals the gate value being renormal

_meta.explanation: The rule 'ExpertChoiceRouter applies route_scale after renormalizing per-token gate weights to sum to 1, so a token routed to a single expert receives a final gate value equal to route_scale (e.g., 2.5), not 1.0.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes token_score_sums[indices] equals the gate value being renormalized, but here the token_score_sums entry is different, so renormalization doesn't yield 1.0 before scaling
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'ExpertChoiceRouter applies route_scale after renormalizing per-token gate weights to sum to 1, so a token routed to a single expert receives a final gate value equal to route_scale (e.g., 2.5), not 1.0.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
